incidents.vector: Hackers breached the city attorney's office network and exfiltrated 7.7 TB of sensitive data.
incidents.impact: Stolen data includes sensitive case details, personal information of LAPD officers, witness names, medical records, and internal affairs investigations — all published online.

incidents.vector: GRU hackers exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, Mikrotik) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users.
incidents.impact: Over 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices compromised across 18,000+ networks. DOJ conducted court-authorized disruption (Operation Masquerade) to remediate U.S. routers. Attackers harvested credentials, emails, and tokens without deploying malware.

incidents.vector: Operators built four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) by compromising routers, webcams, and other IoT devices to launch record-breaking DDoS attacks.
incidents.impact: DOJ, with Canadian and German authorities, dismantled infrastructure behind the botnets. The botnets launched hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks including against DoD networks, often demanding extortion payments.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616, CVSS 9.1) in Fortinet FortiClient EMS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to its KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch. Exploitation began over the Easter weekend when security teams were at reduced capacity. This is the second FortiClient EMS vulnerability disclosed in three weeks.

incidents.victim: University of Mississippi Medical Center, Passaic County NJ, and others
incidents.vector: Medusa operators exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, sometimes one week before public disclosure, moving from initial access to ransomware deployment within 24 hours.
incidents.impact: UMMC hospital was shut down and required FBI and DHS assistance to reopen. Medusa heavily impacts healthcare, education, and financial services in the U.S., U.K., and Australia. North Korean actors have also been observed deploying Medusa ransomware.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a variant of its CanisterWorm malware that detects whether the victim's timezone and locale correspond to Iran, then executes a wiper attack destroying data on Kubernetes clusters or local machines.
incidents.impact: If the wiper detects an Iranian system with Kubernetes access, it destroys data on every node in the cluster. Otherwise, it wipes the local machine. The attack appears to be an opportunistic political statement injected into an ongoing financially motivated campaign.

incidents.vector: Hackers infiltrated the network of the Uffizi, Palazzo Pitti, and Boboli Gardens, took control of a photographic server, and sent a ransom demand to the museum director's personal phone.
incidents.impact: The Uffizi says nothing was stolen and no information was lost due to full backups. Disruption was limited to backup restoration time. Surveillance camera upgrades were accelerated following the incident and the Louvre theft last year.

incidents.vector: Chinese hackers exploited a vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing software's updater to distribute malicious updates across connected endpoints, deploying the Havoc penetration testing tool and ShadowPad malware.
incidents.impact: A compromised TrueConf on-premises server operated by a government IT department served dozens of government entities with the malicious update. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch within two weeks. TrueConf is used by approximately 100,000 organizations globally.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP broke into the European Commission's AWS account by exploiting a compromised API key obtained through the Trivy supply chain attack, exfiltrating 92 GB of data.
incidents.impact: Stolen data included ~52,000 outbound email files, names, email addresses, and some email content belonging to 42 internal clients and at least 29 EU entities. The data appeared on the ShinyHunters dark web site on March 28.

incidents.vector: Attackers used a custom framework called NEXUS Listener to automatically exploit CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell) in Next.js applications and extract cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys from hundreds of compromised hosts.
incidents.impact: High-speed automation enables rapid credential harvesting, establishing persistent unauthenticated access. Centralized aggregation of stolen data allows attackers to map infrastructure for targeted follow-on attacks.

incidents.vector: A researcher publicly released proof-of-concept exploit code for an unpatched Windows zero-day that combines a TOCTOU race condition and path confusion in Windows Defender's signature update system.
incidents.impact: Successful exploitation allows a local user to access the SAM database, obtain password hashes, and gain administrator rights via pass-the-hash. Ransomware gangs and APTs typically deploy such exploits within days of release. Microsoft has not yet patched the flaw.

incidents.vector: Italian surveillance company ASIGINT (subsidiary of SIO) tricked approximately 200 users into downloading a bogus WhatsApp clone rigged with spyware.
incidents.impact: Victims were primarily in Italy. This is the second disruption of spyware activity in Italy in 15 months, following the Paragon spyware exposure in early 2025.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a use-after-free vulnerability (CVE-2026-5281) in Dawn, Chrome's WebGPU implementation — the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to its KEV catalog. Google released an emergency patch. Federal agencies were ordered to update immediately.

incidents.victim: Axios NPM package users (100M+ weekly downloads)
incidents.vector: North Korean hackers (UNC1069) socially engineered the lead Axios maintainer via a fake video call, compromised his npm account, then published malicious versions (1.14.1 and 0.30.4) containing a cross-platform RAT called WAVESHAPER.V2.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions were live for approximately three hours, potentially affecting ~600,000 downloads. The RAT deploys on Windows, macOS, and Linux, performing reconnaissance, establishing persistence, and self-destructing to evade forensics. Described as the most operationally sophisticated supply chain attack on a top-10 npm package.

incidents.victim: NCSC warns: government officials and high-risk individuals
incidents.vector: Russian state actors are using messaging apps (WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram) to target high-risk individuals through phishing, malicious QR codes, device linking exploits, and impersonation of known contacts.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued a joint advisory warning of growing activity targeting government officials' messaging app accounts. Attackers attempt to add devices to accounts, join group chats undetected, and steal login or recovery codes.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler ADC to leak sensitive memory from deployments, mirroring the original Citrix Bleed vulnerability pattern.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. The vulnerability affects the NetScaler Gateway, which serves as the front door for remote users connecting to organizations. Previous Citrix Bleed vulnerabilities were used by ransomware gangs and nation-state hackers against hundreds of organizations.

incidents.vector: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2025-53521, a critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager that was recategorized from DoS to RCE.
incidents.impact: NCSC issued an urgent advisory encouraging immediate investigation for compromise. F5 BIG-IP APM is widely deployed in large enterprises for access management. CISA confirmed active exploitation.

incidents.vector: Attackers gained unauthorized access to Hasbro's network, prompting the company to take some systems offline and activate business continuity plans.
incidents.impact: Hasbro filed an 8-K with the SEC. Full remediation expected to take several weeks. The company is still taking orders and shipping products through backup processes but experiencing delays. Stock fell 3%.

incidents.victim: Open source ecosystem (Trivy, KICS, LiteLLM, Telnyx, 48+ npm packages)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP systematically compromised open-source security tools by poisoning GitHub Actions and PyPI packages, injecting infostealers that harvest cloud tokens, SSH keys, and Kubernetes secrets during routine CI/CD workflows.
incidents.impact: Over 300 GB of data and 500,000 credentials exfiltrated. At least 16 victim organizations published on leak sites. Stolen credentials were then used to breach AWS and Azure environments. The group is now partnering with CipherForce and Vect ransomware groups.

incidents.vector: The Iranian government-connected group Handala launched a wiper attack against Stryker in retaliation over the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.
incidents.impact: The attack damaged order processing, manufacturing, and shipping systems for three weeks. Stryker is now fully operational. The FBI has seized websites associated with Handala and the State Department offered a reward for information on the group.

incidents.vector: German Federal Criminal Police (BKA) identified and publicly named 31-year-old Russian national Daniil Shchukin as the head of the GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations, along with accomplice Anatoly Kravchuk.
incidents.impact: The two are linked to at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion causing over €35 million in damages. GandCrab and REvil collectively extorted over $2 billion from victims. Shchukin is presumed to be in Russia.
